title: Rate-Limited Request Scheduler
description: Given a list of request timestamps (seconds) and a rate limit, determine which requests must be delayed.
problem: Write schedule(timestamps, max_per_second) where timestamps is a sorted list of request times (ints). Return a list of adjusted timestamps such that no more than max_per_second requests share the same integer second, pushing overflow requests to the next second(s), preserving order.
hint: Track a dict counting how many requests are assigned to each second so far. For each timestamp in order, if the count for that second >= max_per_second, try the next second, and so on.
